real_observations.band: near-infrared (4.1 µm, shown red → false colour)
real_observations.credit: NASA, ESA, CSA, STScI, W. Balmer (JHU), L. Pueyo & M. Perrin (STScI)
real_observations.note: The planet is the fuzzy red point labelled “b”, just left of the masked star 51 Eri. Infrared light, false-coloured, not a visible-light photo.

reconstruction_error.note: Perceptual distance from the full-spectrum true colour; how much colour identity survives this instrument's filters.

real_observations.credit: NASA, ESA, CSA, STScI, W. Balmer (JHU), L. Pueyo & M. Perrin (STScI)
real_observations.note: Planet “b” is the blue dot at far left, the outermost of HR 8799’s four planets (~68 AU). Star hidden by the coronagraph; infrared, false-coloured.

reconstruction_error.note: Perceptual distance from the full-spectrum true colour; how much colour identity survives this instrument's filters.

real_observations.credit: NASA, ESA, CSA, STScI, W. Balmer (JHU), L. Pueyo & M. Perrin (STScI)
real_observations.note: Planet “c” is the bluish-white dot at top (~38 AU). Star hidden by the coronagraph; infrared, false-coloured, not a visible-light photo.

reconstruction_error.note: Perceptual distance from the full-spectrum true colour; how much colour identity survives this instrument's filters.

real_observations.credit: NASA, ESA, CSA, STScI, W. Balmer (JHU), L. Pueyo & M. Perrin (STScI)
real_observations.note: Planet “e” is the orange dot nearest the masked star, the innermost of the four (~16 AU). Infrared, false-coloured, not a visible-light photo.

reconstruction_error.note: Perceptual distance from the full-spectrum true colour; how much colour identity survives this instrument's filters.

real_observations.note: Twelve real SPHERE frames (2014–2018): in each, the star sits behind the black mask and Beta Pictoris b is the bright point orbiting it. Infrared, false-coloured.

reconstruction_error.note: Perceptual distance from the full-spectrum true colour; how much colour identity survives this instrument's filters.

real_observations.note: The planet is the faint blue point at lower-left; the star symbol inside the blue circle marks the subtracted stellar position. Thermal infrared, false-coloured.

reconstruction_error.note: Perceptual distance from the full-spectrum true colour; how much colour identity survives this instrument's filters.

real_observations.note: The planet is the white point at upper-right; the star sits behind the black central mask amid blue/orange speckle noise. Near-infrared, false-coloured.
